summary: Tested CSV Contact Import feature for Google Contacts CSV exports. All 18 backend tests passed. Fixed a bug where notes=None was being stored instead of empty string, causing GET /api/contacts/{user_id} to fail with validation error.
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed bug in csv_import.py line 268: notes field was set to None instead of empty string, causing Pydantic validation failure when fetching contacts
CSV preview endpoint correctly parses Google Contacts CSV format with all fields
Duplicate detection by phone works correctly using last 7 digits matching
Primary phone selection correctly prioritizes Mobile > Work > Home labels
Contacts with no phone AND no email are properly skipped during parsing
Import confirm endpoint correctly sets source='csv' and tags=['csv-import']
phones[] and emails[] arrays are correctly stored with label/value structure
organization_name field is properly saved during import and update operations
Smart name splitting works - full name in 'First Name' field is split into first/last
seed_data_creation: Tests use the provided Google Contacts CSV file from https://customer-assets.emergentagent.com/... Test contacts are cleaned up after test run via bulk-delete.
context_for_next_testing_agent: CSV import feature is fully tested. Test file test_csv_import.py covers: preview endpoint (parsing, phones/emails arrays, organization_name, duplicate detection, phone priority selection, skip no-info contacts), confirm endpoint (import creation, source/tags, phones/emails storage, duplicate skipping), contact update with new fields, smart name splitting, and duplicate detection. Bug fixed: notes field in csv_import.py was setting None instead of empty string.

POST /api/contacts/{user_id}/import-csv/.primary_phone_selection: PASS - Mobile > Work > Home priority works

bug_fixed.issue: notes field set to c.get('notes') which returns None when not present
bug_fixed.fix: Changed to c.get('notes') or '' to ensure empty string default
bug_fixed.impact: Fixed Pydantic validation error when fetching contacts list

rca_of_the_issue: The notes field in the CSV import confirm endpoint was being set to None when no notes were provided in the parsed contact. The Contact Pydantic model expects notes to be a string (default ''), not None. This caused a Pydantic validation error when fetching the contacts list, resulting in 500 Internal Server Error.
